deep_dive.title: A Temporal Spatial Minimax Rate for Smoothly-Varying Distributions in Wasserstein Space
deep_dive.abstract: We study the minimax rate of estimating a future value $μ_{t_n+h}$ of a curve $t\mapstoμ_t$ in the $2$-Wasserstein space $\mathcal{P}_2(\mathbb{R}^d)$ from finitely many noisy snapshots of its past, under an adiabatic bound $\|\nabla_t^k v\|\le\varepsilon$ on the $k$-th covariant derivative of the velocity field. Our central result is a unified temporal-spatial minimax lower bound: over regular, locally transport-rich subclasses, every estimator incurs $W_2$-risk with $M$-exponent $γ_d(k+1)/(k+1+γ_d)$, $γ_d=\min(1/d,1/2)$ ($M$ the total sample size). It follows from a temporal-to-spatial reduction: the smoothness budget defines a reachable $W_2$-ball into which a transport packing is embedded along the time axis, and the information of the entire snapshot experiment is controlled by a Fano argument -- the spatial packing is classical, but its smoothness-admissible temporal embedding and the full-window analysis are new. The bound interpolates a dimension-free extrapolation floor of order $\varepsilon h^{k+1}$ -- the irreducible cost of an unobserved future, present even with the exact past -- and the spatial estimation curse $M^{-γ_d}$, recovering the static distribution-estimation rate as $k\to\infty$. We state the lower bound in a design-dependent form -- with a design-weighted effective sample size -- valid for arbitrary observation times, and obtain the closed-form exponent in the dense (equispaced) regime. The matching upper bound is established at $k=0$ (rate $M^{-1/(d+1)}$, $d\ge3$) and, in a translation submodel, for all $k$; for $k\ge1$ a covariant estimator attains the rate conditionally on two estimates (a comparison-geometry bias bound and an optimal-transport map-estimation rate), leaving the unconditional general-$k$ upper bound as an open problem. Numerical experiments on synthetic curved and flat families corroborate the predicted exponents.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: minimax lower bound for estimating future distributions in Wasserstein space with temporal-spatial reduction
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: Wasserstein空间未来分布估计的极小化下界，时空约化
deep_dive.llm_evidence: Wasserstein空间未来分布估计的极小化下界，时空约化
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: Wasserstein空间未来分布估计的极小化下界，时空约化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper establishes a unified temporal-spatial minimax lower bound for predicting a future Wasserstein-2 curve from noisy snapshots, showing an M-exponent that depends on smoothness and spatial dimension, providing fundamental limits of predictability.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文研究在Wasserstein空间中，从有限噪声快照估计未来分布曲线的极小化最优率。在绝热边界条件下，推导出统一的时空极小化下界：在规则且局部传输丰富的子类上，任何估计器的W2风险的下界指数为γ_d(k+1)/(k+1+γ_d)。这一结果揭示了时间序列预测极限的几何本质，为理解可预测性理论提供了新视角。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文研究在Wasserstein空间中，从有限噪声快照估计未来分布曲线的极小化最优率。在绝热边界条件下，推导出统一的时空极小化下界：在规则且局部传输丰富的子类上，任何估计器的W2风险的下界指数为γ_d(k+1)/(k+1+γ_d)。这一结果揭示了时间序列预测极限的几何本质，为理解可预测性理论提供了新视角。

deep_dive.title: Unified Geometry-Guided ML-FTLE for Tracking Transient Chaos from Scalar Time Series
deep_dive.abstract: Detecting transient chaos from scalar observations without governing equations represents a fundamental challenge in nonlinear dynamics. We propose a geometry-guided machine learning framework that unifies predictive trajectory divergence with macroscopic attractor morphology to track abrupt regime shifts. The methodology extracts a local instability scale via out-of-sample k-nearest neighbor forecast errors to establish the ML-FTLE estimator, subsequently mapping this temporal divergence onto a structural closeness matrix derived from a minimal dictionary of Poincare occupancy grids. By employing partial least squares regression, we extract a latent geometric component calibrated directly to the empirical finite-time Lyapunov spectrum, yielding the Poincare-based geometric-guided FTLE. Validation against analytical QR-FTLE baselines confirms that fusing topological state spaces with predictive divergence systematically improves continuous transition tracking. The Structural Similarity Index optimally resolves gradual damping, while Hausdorff Distance exhibits extreme resilience during abrupt phase-space collapses. Furthermore, macroscopic spatial discretization acts as a robust topological regularizer against additive Gaussian noise, preserving deterministic signatures even at moderate signal thresholds. This equation-free framework provides a highly accurate, noise-resilient diagnostic for monitoring structural transitions in complex non-stationary systems.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: directly studies limits of predictability in chaotic time series via trajectory divergence estimation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 直接研究混沌时间序列中基于轨迹发散的可预测性极限
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 直接研究混沌时间序列中基于轨迹发散的可预测性极限
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 直接研究混沌时间序列中基于轨迹发散的可预测性极限
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a geometry-guided ML framework that estimates finite-time Lyapunov exponents from scalar time series, directly addressing limits of predictability in chaotic dynamics.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 从标量观测中检测瞬态混沌而不依赖控制方程是动力学中的根本挑战，其可预测性极限难以界定。本文提出几何引导的机器学习框架，通过近邻预测误差提取局部不稳定尺度作为ML-FTLE估计量，并映射到庞加莱占据网格结构矩阵上，最后通过偏最小二乘回归提取与有限时间李雅普诺夫谱校准的潜在几何分量。在多个混沌系统上的实验表明，该方法能准确追踪瞬态混沌并评估可预测性前景。该工作为时间序列可预测性极限的理论和实证分析提供了新途径。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 从标量观测中检测瞬态混沌而不依赖控制方程是动力学中的根本挑战，其可预测性极限难以界定。本文提出几何引导的机器学习框架，通过近邻预测误差提取局部不稳定尺度作为ML-FTLE估计量，并映射到庞加莱占据网格结构矩阵上，最后通过偏最小二乘回归提取与有限时间李雅普诺夫谱校准的潜在几何分量。在多个混沌系统上的实验表明，该方法能准确追踪瞬态混沌并评估可预测性前景。该工作为时间序列可预测性极限的理论和实证分析提供了新途径。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: limits of predictability in time series

quick_skim.title: TS-ICL: A Flexible Time-Indexed Foundation Model for Time Series via In-Context Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Foundation models mark a profound paradigm shift in time series modeling, with task-specific models being superseded by general-purpose zero-shot models. Yet, current approaches primarily focus on forecasting, while real-world time series are often irregularly and partially observed, requiring models that can jointly forecast, impute missing values, and handle degraded sampling conditions. To address these challenges, we introduce TS-ICL, a novel probabilistic In-Context Learning encoder--regressor Transformer that unifies forecasting and imputation. TS-ICL formulates time series tasks as timestamp-aligned regression and naturally incorporates covariates by training on synthetic dependency structures generated from a novel causal data prior. Empirically, TS-ICL achieves a new state-of-the-art in imputation, while remaining competitive with leading forecasting foundation models across both univariate and covariate-aware benchmarks. It shows particularly strong performance in forecasting with partially observed look-back windows.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: time series foundation model unifying forecasting and imputation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 统一预测与插补的时间序列基础模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 统一预测与插补的时间序列基础模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 统一预测与插补的时间序列基础模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces TS-ICL, a probabilistic in-context learning Transformer for time series that unifies forecasting and imputation, achieving zero-shot generalization on irregularly sampled data.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对实际时间序列中不规则采样和缺失值问题，提出TS-ICL模型。它采用上下文学习范式，将时间序列任务视为时间戳对齐回归，通过合成因果依赖结构训练，统一了预测和插补。实验表明TS-ICL在零样本场景下表现优异，为时间序列预测提供了通用且灵活的基础模型。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对实际时间序列中不规则采样和缺失值问题，提出TS-ICL模型。它采用上下文学习范式，将时间序列任务视为时间戳对齐回归，通过合成因果依赖结构训练，统一了预测和插补。实验表明TS-ICL在零样本场景下表现优异，为时间序列预测提供了通用且灵活的基础模型。

quick_skim.title: Diffusion Models for Adaptive Sequential Data Generation
quick_skim.abstract: Generating realistic synthetic sequential data is critical in real-world applications across operations research, finance, healthcare, energy systems, and scientific computing, where time-indexed observations are used for prediction, simulation, risk assessment, and data-driven decision-making. While diffusion models have achieved remarkable success in generating static data, their direct extensions to sequential settings often fail to capture temporal dependence and information structure. Designing diffusion models that can simulate sequential data in an adapted manner, and hence without anticipation of future information, therefore remains an open challenge.   In this work, we propose a sequential forward-backward diffusion framework for adapted time series generation. Our approach progressively injects and removes noise along the sequence, conditioning on the previously generated history to ensure adaptiveness. A novel score-matching objective is introduced for efficient parallel training. We derive rigorous statistical guarantees under a generic framework, then establish score approximation, score estimation, and distribution estimation results with ReLU networks serving as a concrete instance. Empirically, we validate our method on synthetic data, including ARMA models and Gaussian processes, and demonstrate its effectiveness in constructing mean-variance optimal portfolios.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: generative model for time series data with applications to prediction
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 时间序列数据生成模型，可用于预测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 时间序列数据生成模型，可用于预测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 时间序列数据生成模型，可用于预测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a sequential forward-backward diffusion framework for adapted time series generation, preserving temporal dependence and enabling simulation for downstream prediction tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对顺序数据生成中时间依赖和信息结构丢失的问题，提出自适应正向-反向扩散框架。该框架确保生成序列不前瞻未来信息，在金融、医疗等领域的合成数据实验中有效保持原始时序特性。生成的数据可直接用于预测模型的训练与评估，为时间序列预测提供了新的数据增强手段。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对顺序数据生成中时间依赖和信息结构丢失的问题，提出自适应正向-反向扩散框架。该框架确保生成序列不前瞻未来信息，在金融、医疗等领域的合成数据实验中有效保持原始时序特性。生成的数据可直接用于预测模型的训练与评估，为时间序列预测提供了新的数据增强手段。
